instruction: countdown(n) should return the list from n down to 0 inclusive, e.g. countdown(3) == [3, 2, 1, 0]. There is an off-by-one bug in the loop condition: it stops one step too early and never appends 0. Fix the boundary so 0 is included.

files.test_countdown.py:
from countdown import countdown


def test_countdown():
    assert countdown(3) == [3, 2, 1, 0]
    assert countdown(0) == [0]
    assert countdown(1) == [1, 0]
